hero.title: Browse, add, map.
hero.subtitle: One screen with hotels, restaurants, activities and transport — curated for your destination. Hit + to add to the trip, watch it land on the shared map.
hero.ctaSubtext: Free · no credit card · 30 seconds to start

intro.title: The research-to-trip pipeline, broken.
paragraphs: Planning a group trip should be a conversation, not a spreadsheet. But here you are: 47 browser tabs open, three group chats with different lists, a Notion doc no one updates, and at 11pm the night before you're still arguing whether the rooftop bar is worth the trek across town. The 'what should we do' question — supposed to be the fun part — turns into the part that breaks groups.

The problem isn't a lack of information. It's the opposite. Every city has 10,000 things to do, and the internet promises all of them are must-see. Sorting signal from noise — and getting four to fifteen people to agree on what made the cut — is where most trips quietly fall apart.

Explorer is built for that exact moment. One screen. Four tabs: hotels, restaurants, activities, transport. A curated card grid for your destination. Hit + to add to the trip, see it land on the shared map. Your group does the same, the consensus places float up. Twenty minutes, one mapped shortlist.

painPoints.title: Why discovery is broken
painPoints.subtitle: Most groups spend more time fighting the planning than enjoying the trip.

items.title: The tab graveyard
items.description: 35+ open tabs, every one promising the best version of 'best things to do in [city]'. You'll close them tonight, open them again tomorrow. Nothing got decided.

items.title: The group chat list nobody updates
items.description: Sarah suggested 6 places, Marc added 3, Alex liked all of them, the bride-to-be hasn't seen any of it. You'll bring it up at brunch. Maybe.

items.title: Is this actually good or just touristy?
items.description: Tripadvisor is full of paid reviews, Reddit threads are 4 years old, Instagram is #ad. You end up at the same overcrowded restaurant everyone else picked too.

items.title: The 'I saved it somewhere' black hole
items.description: 14 Maps stars, 28 Instagram saves, 6 TikTok bookmarks, a screenshot in your camera roll. You'll never find any of them again the day you arrive.

howItWorks.title: How Explorer fixes it
howItWorks.subtitle: One screen, four tabs, a real shared shortlist at the end.

items.title: Four tabs, not a firehose
items.description: Switch between hotels, restaurants, activities, transport with a single click. The cards adapt to the tab — accommodation shows price-per-night, transport shows route + duration. No more comparing apples to plane tickets.

items.title: Add with one tap
items.description: Every card has a + button. Tap it, the place locks into your trip — green check, no double-confirmation. Your selections sync to the rest of the group in real-time.

items.title: Map view, side-by-side
items.description: Cards on the left, interactive map on the right (or below on mobile). Hover a card, the pin lights up on the map. Pick by proximity, not by tab order.

items.title: Group consensus surfaces
items.description: Everyone sees the same Explorer. When multiple members add the same place, it gets a 'crowd favorite' marker. The places everyone secretly agreed on rise to the top — no debate needed.

animationShowcase.title: See it in action
animationShowcase.subtitle: The real Explorer interface — same tabs, same cards, same + button. Switch types, watch the grid update.
animationShowcase.caption: Auto-playing demo · this is what you'll use

mapPreview.title: Everything you added, mapped
mapPreview.subtitle: Every + tap drops a pin on the shared map. Open it the day you arrive, pick what's closest, walk to it.
mapPreview.caption: Interactive map · synced with your trip

useCases.title: When Explorer earns its keep
useCases.subtitle: Built for the trips where 'just look it up' doesn't scale.

items.title: City breaks (3–5 days)
items.description: Long weekends in Lisbon, Berlin, NYC. You don't have time to research — you have time to do. Explorer narrows 500 ideas into 20 in fifteen minutes.

items.title: Group trips (4–15 people)
items.description: Bachelorette weekends, friend reunions, family trips. Everyone adds their picks from their phone, the consensus floats up. Bypass the group-chat democracy.

items.title: Multi-city & road trips
items.description: 5 cities in 10 days = 5 explores. Save places per city, hit them when you arrive. Your itinerary builds itself around what the group actually wants.

page.seoDescription: Discover amazing places with our exploration mode. Swipe to explore restaurants, museums, and activities, save your favorites.
page.ctaSubtitle: Discover thousands of amazing places for your next trip

faqItems.question: How does Weplanify's Explore mode work?
faqItems.answer: Explore mode lets you discover places (restaurants, museums, activities) by swiping like a dating app. Swipe right to save, left to skip.

faqItems.question: Are places filtered based on my destination?
faqItems.answer: Yes, the Explorer only shows places relevant to your destination and travel dates.

faqItems.question: Where do the recommendations come from?
faqItems.answer: Our recommendations combine multiple sources: Weplanify traveler reviews, Google Places data, TripAdvisor, and our local curators.

faqItems.question: Can I use Explorer offline?
faqItems.answer: Yes! Download places from your destination before you leave.

faqItems.question: How do I add an explored place to my itinerary?
faqItems.answer: When you swipe right (like), the place is saved to your favorites. A simple tap lets you add it to your itinerary.
